smartlead_campaign: OR #4 | Ecom Leads | Scroll Stopper | NO Personlization

magnet.report_label: Scroll-Stopper Sheet (the 3 ads we wrote them)

thread.subject: made these for you
thread.body_text:
hi Ellie, spent a few minutes on the Twisted Pepper site and I'm a little annoyed we're not working together yet haha.

but that's not why I reached out. I handle Meta ads for ecom brands, and instead of sending a BS pitch, I built you some.

built 3 ad concepts from Twisted Pepper's own products, plus a world-class brand playbook I pulled from your site and customer reviews. it's all done and waiting on my side.

should I send it over?

- Kyle

thread.subject: RE: made these for you
thread.body_text:
Ellie - the thing most brands notice first isn't the ads, it's the brand playbook. it maps your voice, the exact words your customers use in reviews, and what you can and can't say in ad copy.

it's ready to go with the mockups.

 want me to pass both along?

- Kyle
please send me the brand playbook please

here you go Ellie - 3 ad mockups built from Twisted Pepper's own products, plus your brand playbook .

 the mockups show the angles, the playbook is the part most brands actually keep - your voice, the words your customers use, and what you can and can't say in ad copy.

 if any of them click, the quickest way to see how I'd actually run them is a 30 min call. → Calendar 

 - Kyle

copy.tldr: Twisted Pepper is a real gourmet dip and seasoning brand with a strong founder story but a $9.99 median price and no ads running, so this call is about starting clean with bundles, not fixing a broken account.

deep_dive.what_they_are: A handcrafted gourmet food brand run by founder Ellie Hughes since 2005. They sell dip mixes, soup mixes, BBQ rubs and seasonings direct to consumers and to wholesale accounts. Built on Shopify.
deep_dive.what_they_sell: 103 products. Dip mixes, soup mixes, BBQ rubs, seasoning blends, plus multi-packs and gift boxes. Single items run cheap ($5.95 to $12.99). The multi-packs and collections are where the real money sits ($22.99 to $36). Median price is $9.99, which we are using as the stand-in for average order value.
deep_dive.positioning: Approachable gourmet. Fine-restaurant flavor made fast and easy for busy home cooks. Gluten free, no MSG, low sodium. Not mass-market bland.
deep_dive.who_buys: Busy home cooks who want bold flavor without effort, and gift-givers buying for the soup or dip lover in their life. Gifting is a big angle given the gift boxes and holiday mentions in reviews.
customer_voice: I am absolutely addicted to your soups and dips/cheese ball mixes. To be honest - all those I bought from you never went for gifts! They went in our stomachs, and to share with extended family and friends over the holidays.
My husband Loves the Pig Sweat Rub. He sprinkles on his bacon while cooking, and anything pork! I order 4 jars at time.
strengths: Real founder story and a clear voice. Ellie has been at this since 2005 and the brand sounds like a person, not a factory.
Strong bundle and gift lineup already built. The 4-packs at $22.99 and 3-pack collections at $30 to $36 solve the low price problem before we even start.
gaps: Almost no visible reviews on the site. A few products show 3 to 7 reviews, most show none. Hard to build trust in an ad with nothing to point to.
No Meta ads running that we can find, so there is no history to learn from. We start from zero. Shipping policy page also says 'Coming Soon', which looks unfinished to a buyer.

game_plan.headline: Start from the Scroll-Stopper ads, lead with bundles not single jars, and prove one winner before we spend big.

phases.goal: Prove that paid ads can bring in a sale profitably at all. Find one ad and one product that work.
campaigns: One testing campaign with the 3 Scroll-Stopper ads we already wrote, each pointed at a bundle page
A simple retargeting campaign for people who visit but do not buy
lead_products: 4 Pack Dipping Madness Dip Mixes ($22.99) goes live first. The reviews and homepage lean hard on jalapeno and bold heat, this bundle is all jalapeno flavors, and at $22.99 it carries ad costs far better than a $5.95 single jar.
Baked Potato soup is called out as a 'Best Seller' on the Comfort Soup Collection 3 pack ($30), so the soup collection is our second test product.
phases.creative: Run the 3 ads from the Scroll-Stopper Sheet as-is. Lead with the boldest jalapeno angle first because the brand's own reviews and homepage push heat and 'big flavors'. Point every ad at a bundle page, never a single jar.
phases.budget: Start around $40 to $50 a day across the tests.
phases.what_success_looks_like: At least one ad gets clicks at a sensible cost and drives a few sales. We are not chasing scale yet, we just want proof one angle works.

phases.goal: Put more money behind whatever won in month one and widen who we show it to.
campaigns: Scale the winning ad and winning product into its own campaign
Add a broader cold audience and a lookalike built off early buyers and site visitors
lead_products: Whichever bundle won in Phase 1. If jalapeno won, expand into the Spiced Up 3 Pack Seasonings ($29) as a close cousin.
Keep the soup collection running if it showed life, since soup buyers tend to reorder.
phases.creative: Refresh the winning ad with 2 to 3 new versions of the same angle. Add a customer quote to the ad once we have gathered a few real ones (see discovery).
phases.budget: Step up to roughly $80 to $120 a day, only on what is already working.
phases.what_success_looks_like: The winning ad holds its performance as we spend more, and the wider audience still buys.

phases.goal: Scale the proven play and add a clear second product so we are not resting on one.
campaigns: Launch a gift-focused campaign around the Gift Boxes and 3-pack collections
lead_products: The proven bundle stays the workhorse.
Gift Boxes and the 3-pack collections ($30 to $36) as the second product, because reviews already show people buy these to share and gift.
phases.creative: Add a gifting angle ('the gift for the soup or dip lover who has everything'). Build on the free shipping over $40 offer, which pushes people toward bundles anyway.
phases.budget: Scale toward $150 a day and up, guided by what stays profitable.
phases.what_success_looks_like: Two products are selling from ads, spend is going up without the return falling apart, and there is a gift push ready for the holidays.

estimates.label: Starting budget
estimates.basis: enough to get roughly 30 to 40 clicks a day at typical costs in this food category, which is the floor for learning what works before we spend more

estimates.label: Order value we are planning around
estimates.basis: we are steering ads at the 4-pack ($22.99) and 3-pack collections ($30 to $36) instead of the $9.99 median single item, because bundles are the only way the math works for paid ads here

estimates.label: Revenue we would aim to add
estimates.value: We would not put a dollar figure on this yet
estimates.basis: we have no access to their current sales, margin or conversion rate, so any number would be a guess. We need the discovery answers first.

assumptions: Ads point at bundle pages, not single $5.95 to $9.99 jars, or the numbers will not work.
The website converts well enough today, meaning it takes payment cleanly and the shipping page gets finished.
Ellie has margin on the $22.99 bundles to afford customer acquisition. We need to confirm this on the call.
There is enough inventory to handle more orders if ads start working.

questions.question: Are you running any paid ads right now, on Meta or anywhere else?
questions.listening_for: We could not find any Meta ads. If she confirms none are running, this is a clean start, not a rescue. If she has run some before, ask what happened.

questions.question: Who handles your marketing today, you or someone on your team?
questions.listening_for: If it is all Ellie, she is stretched thin and time is a real objection. If she has help, find out who and whether they have tried ads.

questions.question: Have you ever tried Facebook or Instagram ads before, and how did it go?
questions.listening_for: Any past bad experience shapes her trust. Listen for whether it failed on budget, on the product, or because nobody managed it properly.

questions.question: On a $22.99 four-pack, roughly how much is left after your product and packing costs?
questions.listening_for: This is the whole deal. If there is healthy margin on the bundles, ads can work. If margin is thin even on bundles, be honest that paid ads will be hard.

questions.question: When people land on your site, do they usually buy the bundles or the single jars?
questions.listening_for: If they mostly buy $6 singles, average order value is too low for ads and we must push bundles hard. If bundles already sell, we are in good shape.

questions.question: Out of everything you make, what actually sells the most?
questions.listening_for: Confirms whether our jalapeno bundle and Baked Potato soup guesses are right. If her real bestseller is different, the lead product in the plan changes.

questions.question: I noticed most products have no reviews on the site. Do you have reviews or customer photos somewhere we are not seeing, like email or social?
questions.listening_for: We need social proof for ads. If she has reviews sitting in email or on Facebook, we can pull them into ads. If there is truly nothing, month one has to work harder.

questions.question: Your shipping policy page says 'Coming Soon' and free shipping kicks in at $40. Is the shipping and checkout all working properly right now?
questions.listening_for: A broken or unclear checkout kills ad money. Make sure the buying experience is finished before we send paid traffic to it.

questions.question: Do you get many people finding you on Google by searching things like 'gluten free dip mix' or 'chili seasoning'?
questions.listening_for: People clearly search for named seasonings and mixes, so there is a real Google opening here. Listen for whether she already ranks or gets search traffic. If yes, Google and SEO are worth raising.

questions.question: How big is your wholesale and fundraiser side, and do you want more of it?
questions.listening_for: She mentions wholesale and fundraisers. That is a different growth path. If she wants it, note it, but keep this call focused on Meta first.

objections.objection: My products are cheap, only $6 to $10, ads will never pay off.
objections.answer: You are right that a single jar is too cheap for ads. That is exactly why we point every ad at your bundles. The 4-packs are $22.99 and the collections run $30 to $36, and your free shipping over $40 already nudges people to buy more. We build the whole plan around your higher-value orders, not the single jars.

objections.objection: I have never run ads and I do not want to waste money.
objections.answer: That is why month one is small on purpose, around $40 to $50 a day, just to prove one ad and one product can work. We do not scale spend until something is actually working. You are not betting big on day one.

objections.objection: I do not have many reviews to show people.
objections.answer: We noticed. The good news is the reviews you do have are strong, people say they are addicted and buy four jars at a time. We lead with your founder story and bold flavor first, and as sales come in we gather more reviews to feed straight back into the ads. Part of month one is fixing this, not ignoring it.

objections.objection: I am too busy to manage this on top of everything else.
objections.answer: You do not manage it, that is our job. We already wrote the three ads you saw and built the playbook. We handle the setup, the testing and the reporting. You just tell us when we get something wrong about the brand.

red_flags.flag: Median price is only $9.99 and many products sell for $5.95 to $9.
red_flags.why_it_matters: Single items are too cheap to carry paid ad costs. The plan only works if we push bundles and gift sets. If she resists that, the math breaks.

red_flags.flag: Almost no reviews visible on the site.
red_flags.why_it_matters: No social proof makes ads harder to trust and lowers the chance someone buys from a cold ad. We need to find or gather reviews early.
An unfinished shipping page makes buyers hesitate and can hurt conversions. This should be fixed before we spend on traffic.

red_flags.flag: No ad history and no analytics access for us.
red_flags.why_it_matters: We are starting blind with no past data to learn from. That is fine, but it means month one is genuinely a test, and we should not promise revenue numbers we cannot back up.

other_channel_openings: Google Search is a real opening. People search for specific things like 'gluten free dip mix', 'chili seasoning' and 'BBQ rub', which this brand makes by name. Worth raising once Meta is proven.
SEO on their recipe pages. They already publish recipes tied to their products, which is a natural fit for search traffic over time.
